cero: noun. A large scombroid food fish (Scomberomorus regalis) found chiefly in the West Indies.
cider: noun. An alcoholic, often sparkling (carbonated) beverage made from fermented apples.

cor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Cornish.

core: noun. In general usage, an essential part of a thing surrounded by other essential things.

credit: verb. To believe; to put credence in.

edict: noun. A proclamation of law or other authoritative command.

orc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orma.
ore: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orejón.
ort: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Adivasi Odia.
record: noun. An item of information put into a temporary or permanent physical medium.
recto: noun. The front side of a flat object which is to be examined visually, as for reading, such as a sheet, leaf, coin or medal.
rector: noun. In the Anglican Church, a cleric in charge of a parish and who owns the tithes of it.
red: noun. The colour of the setting sun, blood, and strawberries; the colour which is evoked by the longest visible wavelengths (between about
rei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reli.
ret: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Retta.
rice: noun. Cereal plants, Oryza sativa of the grass family whose seeds are used as food.
riced: adjective. Made into or consisting of finely minced pieces resembling rice
ricer: noun. A person, especially a Native American, who cultivates and harvests rice.
rid: verb. Followed by of: to free (oneself or someone, or a place) from an annoyance or hindrance.
ride: verb. To transport oneself by sitting on and directing a horse, later also a bicycle etc.
riot: noun. A tumultuous disturbance of the public peace by a large group of people, often involving violence or damage to property.

tire: verb. To become sleepy or weary.
tired: verb. simple past and past participle of tire
